Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: 1. **Understand User Goal:** The user wants me to analyze a provided event log for a Customer Support Ticket Resolution process. I need to: - Identify cases with significantly longer resolution times. - Determine potential root causes (escalations, waiting times, delays). - Explain how these factors increase cycle times. - Provide insights/recommendations to address bottlenecks.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2. **Analyze the Data:** Ill calculate the total resolution time for each case from Receive Ticket to Close Ticket. Ill also look at intermediate durations to spot delays. *Case 101:* - Start: 2024-03-01 08:00 - End: 2024-03-01 10:15 - Duration: 2 hours 15 minutes - Path: Receive -> Triage (10m) -> Assign L1 (10m) -> Investigate (40m) -> Resolve (1h) -> Close (15m) - No escalation. Smooth flow. *Case 102:* - Start: 2024-03-01 08:05 - End: 2024-03-02 09:15 - Duration: ~25 hours 10 minutes - Path: Receive -> Triage (25m) -> Assign L1 (30m) -> **Escalate to Level-2 Agent** (2.5h) -> Investigate (2.5h) -> Resolve (next day 19h later) -> Close (15m) - Key delays: Gap between Assign L1 and Escalate (2.5h), but huge gap between Investigate (14:00) and Resolve (next day 09:00) -> ~19 hours. Also, escalation happened.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: *Case 103:* - Start: 2024-03-01 08:10 - End: 2024-03-01 09:30 - Duration: 1 hour 20 minutes [...] *Case 104:* [...] Key delays: Assign L1 to Investigate (3.5h wait). Investigate to Resolve (~19h wait). No escalation mentioned, but still crosses day boundary. *Case 105:* [...] Key delays: Escalation, then massive gap before second investigation (~29h), then another ~19h gap to resolution. Longest case.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 3. **Identify Long Cases:** - Case 103: 1h 20m (Baseline/Fast) - Case 101: 2h 15m (Baseline/Normal) - Case 104: ~24h 10m (Long) - Case 102: ~25h 10m (Long) - Case 105: ~49h 5m (Very Long) Significantly longer: 102, 104, 105.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4. **Analyze Root Causes & Patterns:** - *Escalation:* Cases 102 and 105 involved escalation to Level-2. This correlates with longer times, but Case 104 didnt escalate and still took ~24h. So escalation is a factor, but not the only one. - *Overnight/Weekend Gaps:* All long cases (102, 104, 105) have a gap between Investigate Issue and Resolve Ticket that spans overnight [...] - *Process Bottleneck:* The transition from Investigate to Resolve consistently shows a multi-hour/overnight gap in long cases. This implies either: a) Agents work standard business hours and tickets arent resolved same-day if investigation finishes late. b) Lack of SLA monitoring for same-day resolution. c) Level-2 queue backlog.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: 5. **Structure the Response:** - **Part 1: Identification of Long Cases** (Calculate and list durations, highlight outliers) - **Part 2: Root Cause Analysis** (Break down by factors: Escalations, Overnight Gaps, Pre/Post Activity Delays, Queue/Handoff inefficiencies) - **Part 3: Impact on Cycle Times & Recommendations** [...]

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 6. **Draft - Section by Section (Mental Refinement):** *Part 2: Root Causes* - **Overnight/Non-Business Hour Gaps:** The most consistent pattern. [...] - **Lack of Same-Day Resolution Protocol:** [...] *Part 3: Impact & Recommendations* - Explain how these compound: Escalation + queue wait + overnight pause = multi-day cycles. - Recommendations: 1. Implement strict SLAs [...] 5. Track active work time vs wait time separately [...]

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 7. **Self-Correction/Verification during drafting:** - *Check calculations:* 101: 08:00 to 10:15 = 2h15m. Correct. [...] - *Check patterns:* All long cases cross day boundaries. The gap between Investigate and Resolve is consistently ~18-19 hours. This strongly points to end-of-day cutoff. - *Escalation impact:* [...] - *Structure matches prompt:* [...] Everything looks solid.
